one_liner: MELI at $1,807 has RSI 74.7 (overbought) and MACD histogram +16.9 (elevated) — the LatAm e-commerce/fintech giant is extended near its all-time highs; watch for a pullback to the $1,650 EMA50 before adding.

price_action.intraday_story: MELI pressed to $1,809.38 near its Wyckoff resistance at $1,887.22. Volume at 385K is very low. RSI at 74.7 signals the stock is overbought at these levels. Holding near all-time highs but overextended.

news_catalyst.headline: Is a Stock Split Still on the Table for MELI in 2026?
news_catalyst.summary: MELI is trading below $2,000 and analyst discussion around a potential stock split for accessibility. No fundamental catalyst today. The LatAm e-commerce and fintech story remains intact — Mercado Pago is growing rapidly as a payments business.

technical.macd_signal: Strongly bullish — MACD histogram +16.948, but elevated
technical.summary: RSI 74.7 is overbought and MACD at +16.9 is at an extreme. Stock is above EMA50 ($1,648) but approaching Wyckoff resistance at $1,887. Watch for a pullback to $1,650 EMA50 for a better entry. No DCF (model not feasible for this name).
technical.wyckoff_narrative: Extended uptrend with RSI overbought. Watching for a reversion to EMA50 at $1,648 as a buying opportunity.

valuation.summary: DCF model not feasible. MELI trades at a premium multiple justified by its LatAm e-commerce and Mercado Pago fintech dominance.

key_strengths: Dominant LatAm e-commerce platform with 100M+ active buyers
Mercado Pago fintech generating high-growth payments revenue
key_concerns: C grade (0.55) reflects high valuation vs. revenue base
Currency risk across Brazil, Argentina, Mexico
fundamentals.summary: C grade but business quality is better than the score suggests — the model likely penalizes the high P/E. Watch for a better entry on a pullback.

risks: RSI 74.7 and approaching Wyckoff resistance $1,887 — extended near-term
LatAm currency devaluation risk, especially Argentine peso
